danger_signals.visual_form: Thematic hazard design (ice, fire, plant, etc.) matching boss identity
danger_signals.meaning: Visual telegraphing of boss threat type and recommended weapon strategy

danger_signals.visual_form: Rapid movement, spinning animations, projectile emissions
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat state; indicates when minions are attacking

danger_signals.visual_form: Sharp geometric obstacles placed in platforming paths
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate damage zones requiring navigation skill

danger_signals.visual_form: Sparse health pickups in late-game areas
danger_signals.meaning: Resource depletion indicates escalating difficulty and danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleting health gauge during boss encounters
danger_signals.meaning: Boss damage accumulation; visual feedback of progress toward victory

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating items after defeating enemies or finding hidden caches
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; safe moment to recover health
Safe zone before major combat encounter
Tactical resource restoration for weapon use

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue and red primary color scheme
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mega Man's blue helmet and arm cannon design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clean, orderly base environments; peaceful Light Labs aesthetic

faction_indicators.color_identity: Varied per master (Blizzard Man: cyan/blue, Flame Man: red/orange, Plant Man: green, Knight Man: gold/armor, Yamato Man: white/red, Centaur Man: purple, Tomahawk Man: brown/red, Wind Man: cyan)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Each Robot Master has distinct thematic visual design matching their weapon/ability
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemies within each stage adopt color palettes matching the Robot Master's aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage backgrounds and obstacle design reflect Robot Master theme (ice caverns for Blizzard Man, volcanic areas for Flame Man, etc.)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Mr. X fortress design; later revealed as Dr. Wily disguise
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mr. X Castle features heavier, more aggressive enemy variants and mechanical aesthetics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortress environment is distinctly mechanical and dark, contrasting with Robot Master stages; spike patterns and industrial design language

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Robot Tournament setting with themed Robot Master stages; colorful, varied environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robot Masters act independently; their stages are thematic rather than fortress-like

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker color palette introduction; Robot Masters' expressions and poses shift to more aggressive stances in stage select
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies become more aggressive and coordinated; stage hazards increase in difficulty

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to Mr. X Castle with industrial, mechanical aesthetic; heavy use of dark colors and mechanical spike patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy density increases; mini-boss encounters precede final boss; visual complexity escalates

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mr. X Castle transforms/extends into Dr. Wily's fortress with familiar design language from previous games
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Final boss fight introduces Wily's characteristic combat patterns; defeat ends with Dr. Wily imprisonment

environmental_storytelling.technique: Thematic stage design matching Robot Master identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Blizzard Man's stage features icy platforms and snow-themed obstacles; Flame Man's stage has lava pits and fire hazards

environmental_storytelling.technique: Color palette shifts signaling faction control
environmental_storytelling.example: Robot Master stages use bright, distinct colors; Mr. X's fortress employs darker industrial grays and blacks

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy placement and behavior reflecting world state
environmental_storytelling.example: Early stages feature scattered minions; fortress stages have dense enemy formations suggesting organized military structure
Dr. Light's base has clean, orderly aesthetics; Mr. X fortress features mechanical, fortress-like architecture with visible weaponry and defenses
Early Robot Master stages have manageable hazards; Mr. X/Wily fortress combines multiple hazard types in complex patterns

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Simple movement patterns, no projectile emission; generally ignore Mega Man at distance
Charge toward Mega Man; emit projectiles; animation speeds increase
Appear static or execute calm animations in stage-select screen; postures suggest their thematic nature
Rapid animations; weapon-specific attack patterns; aggressive movement toward Mega Man; telegraphed vulnerability windows
Boss arena combat with chair-based attacks; mechanical, angular animations suggesting artificial nature
Shifts to signature Wily attack patterns from previous games; visual tells indicate phase transitions

notes: Mega Man 6 uses visual theming as primary world-state communication device. The contrast between colorful, thematically-varied Robot Master stages and the dark, industrial Mr. X/Wily fortress clearly marks narrative progression from tournament setting to invasion crisis. Color identity strongly correlates faction affiliation. Enemy sprite animation speed and density increases correlate with danger escalation. The game lacks explicit narrative UI; story progression is communicated almost entirely through environmental and enemy visual design.
